concerns.text: Task T017 implements imputation using 'Structure-based KNN (Morgan fingerprints)' and 'Euclidean distance in fingerprint space'. This semantically weakens Spec FR-003, which explicitly mandates 'Euclidean distance in stoichiometry space (normalized element counts)'. The task replaces the required stoichiometry-based metric with a structure-based proxy without a formal spec amendment, violating the constraint that the imputation method must match the spec's definition of 'stoichiometry similarity'.

Task T014 explicitly overrides Spec FR-002 by omitting `synthesis_condition` from alignment logic. While the Plan.md documents this scope adjustment, the task description states 'Explicitly override Spec FR-002' rather than implementing the Plan's revised alignment logic (composition + surface_facet only). This creates a semantic drift where the task acknowledges a violation of the spec rather than implementing the valid, revised requirement defined in the Plan. The task should implement the Plan's logic directly without framing it as an override of the spec.

Task T028a implements a Shapiro-Wilk test on 'paired differences' (XGBoost error - Baseline error) to decide between t-test and Wilcoxon. Spec FR-005 requires the Shapiro-Wilk test to be performed on the 'distribution of absolute errors' (singular model errors) to check normality assumptions for the paired test. The task shifts the statistical object of the normality test from the error distribution to the difference distribution, which is a semantic weakening of the statistical protocol defined in FR-005.

Task T037 verifies SC-003 by training a 'reduced model' on top 5 descriptors. Spec SC-003 explicitly requires the model to be 'trained independently' (implying independent hyperparameter tuning) to measure feature redundancy. The task description does not explicitly mandate independent hyperparameter tuning for the reduced model, risking a semantic drift where the reduced model is merely the full model with masked features, which would invalidate the SC-003 measurement of redundancy.
